missing: Total Eurail Pass volume sold via institutional / B2B channels (in pass-units and in EUR) — Eurail does not publish a B2B-vs-D2C split, and analyst sources (Phocuswright, Skift) have no Eurail-specific corporate-rail breakout.
searched: Eurail B.V. annual filings via creditsafe / pitchbook / tracxn
LinkedIn Pulse Jodi Ten Bohmer essay on Eurail.com
finding: No public figure exists. The only sized institutional program is DiscoverEU (~36,000 EU-funded passes/year) — that's an EU-Commission-funded youth program, not commercial B2B. Saxorail is ~700/year. Other named institutional partners (KIIS, AIFS, Linking Lives, Cipra) are study-abroad / NGO — sub-1,000 passes each as a reasonable upper bound. Eurail's 'B2B' headline number is dominated by OTA wholesale, which is leisure distribution.
recommend_ask: Ask Eurail directly in technical session: (a) what % of pass volume goes through institutional partnerships vs OTAs vs direct web; (b) is any of that volume corporate-employer (not gov/edu/NGO); (c) is corporate-travel a strategic priority for the next 24 months.

missing: Whether any TMC (AmEx GBT, BCD, CWT, FCM, Reed & Mackay, TravelPerk, Egencia) has a contract or wholesaler relationship with Eurail B.V. for the Eurail Pass product.
searched: Eurail press release mentioning TMC / GBT / BCD / Concur / Egencia / TravelPerk
Business Travel News Europe coverage of pass distribution
finding: Zero hits. The corporate rail-distribution rails (Trainline Partner Solutions, Evolvi, SilverRail, SAP Concur) all bypass Eurail entirely and route to operators (SNCF, DB, Eurostar, Trenitalia). The Eurail Pass product appears to be deliberately outside the corporate-travel-distribution stack.
recommend_ask: Confirm with Eurail: is there an explicit decision NOT to route through TMCs, or is it incidental? Is the cooperative governance the constraint?

missing: Direct fetch of r/Eurail and r/Interrail 'new' feeds for verbatim Stage 8 posts
searched: reddit.com new-sort fetch attempted; web.archive.org alternatives; site:reddit.com search via WebSearch
reason_unfound: WebFetch blocked on reddit.com; WebSearch site:reddit.com returned no usable links for the specific 'just got back / trip report' query patterns
note: Reddit corpus exists but not reachable via this subagent's tooling. Recommend: (a) ask Eurail's social-listening vendor for the export, (b) use a SerpAPI-style indirection in a follow-up pass.

missing: Eurail post-trip CRM email signal — does Eurail send a 'rate your trip' or 'leave a review' email after pass expiry?
searched: eurail.com newsletter pages, Trustpilot review patterns, community forum, Phocuswire, generic CRM-pattern search
reason_unfound: No public artifact found. Eurail customer-service responses to Trustpilot reviews exist (reactive), but no proactive post-trip CRM trigger is in the public record
note: Direct ask for technical session: does Eurail run any post-trip CRM trigger today? What's the open rate? What's the NPS instrument if any?

reason_unfound: WebSearch returned a mix of recap and planning videos but TikTok hashtag analytics endpoints are not directly reachable
note: Strong qualitative signal that recap content exists at volume; quantitative split needs a TikTok-API or social-listening export from Eurail.

missing: Eurail repeat-purchase rate / referral attribution / LTV by cohort
searched: company-website, press releases, magazine, annual-style content
reason_unfound: Not publicly disclosed; Eurail is a private B.V., not a listed company. Trainline (listed plc) discloses repeat user 33% on AI assistant; Eurail equivalent not public
note: Direct ask for technical session: Stage 8 telemetry is the foundation for any Stage 8 AI ROI claim.

claim: České dráhy (ČD) Czech Railways customer-facing AI status — no public signal across English or Czech rail/tech press as of 2026-05-06.

WebSearch: 'CD Cesky drah AI sztuczna inteligencja' (cross-language)

recommendation: Ask in technical session: does Eurail have visibility into ČD's AI roadmap? Re-check ČD AI status quarterly.

claim: Whether Eurail B.V. has signed up as a UIC RTMDE consumer (subscriber) is not in the public record — searches across UIC.org, Hit Rail, Eurail press, Xebia partner content and major industry press surface no announcement
recommended_ask: Has Eurail signed an RTMDE consumption contract with Hit Rail? If so, when does it go live, and what is the rollout plan to surface RTMDE data in the Rail Planner App?

claim: The dated roadmap for extending real-time timetable coverage from 6 to N carriers in the Rail Planner App is genuinely undisclosed — no Eurail community post, press release, or partner case study between Dec 2024 and May 2026 names a target carrier or date
searched: Eurail CD MAV DSB VR real-time
recommended_ask: Which carriers join the Rail Planner App's real-time set in H2 2026 and 2027? What's the criterion (data quality, contract, technical readiness)?

claim: Whether Eurail's 6-of-35 architecture is bilateral feed integrations (each carrier pushes to Eurail) or runs through an intermediary (Hafas, OpenTransport, Hit Rail eTCD) is not publicly stated; only the pull-not-push semantic is confirmed
searched: Eurail bilateral data feed carrier API
recommended_ask: What's the actual integration topology — direct carrier-to-Eurail webhooks, a third-party aggregator, or eTCD/RTMDE? What's the SLA and data-quality variance per carrier?

claim: Whether Eurail B.V. has any role in the CER 'custom-built information exchange platform' announced operational on 19 March 2026, or is purely a consumer of its outputs, is not disclosed
recommended_ask: Is Eurail a contributor or consumer of the CER information exchange platform? Has Eurail been at the implementation working-group level, or is it downstream of the operators' work?

claim: Eurail's compliance plan for TEL TSI (Reg. EU 2026/253, in force 2 March 2026) is not disclosed — the regulation creates obligations on rail operators and ticket retailers but Eurail's status as 'retailer' under the regulation has not been publicly confirmed or denied
recommended_ask: Does Eurail consider itself a 'ticket retailer' under TEL TSI? What's the compliance project plan, and how does it intersect with the Rail Planner App's real-time feed roadmap?

question: Does Eurail run any internal LLM gateway / Bedrock / OpenAI / Anthropic deployment for engineering or analyst use?
searched: WebSearch combinations of 'Eurail' x {Bedrock, SageMaker, OpenAI, Anthropic, MLflow, vector store, embedding}
conference rosters PyData Amsterdam / MLOps World / PyCon DE 2025
outcome: No public signal of any internal LLM gateway, model registry, vector store, or AI platform. The Platform Engineer JD's 'explore AI-assisted tools' is the strongest internal-AI signal in the public record. Recommend asking in technical session.

question: Are there Eurail-affiliated employees with AI/ML/data-scientist titles below the surfaced LinkedIn roster?
searched: site:linkedin.com/in 'Eurail' x {data engineer, ML engineer, AI engineer, data scientist}
outcome: Cannot confirm or rule out due to LinkedIn login wall. Only 4 employees publicly visible on the company page; ~250 not surfaced. The absence of AI hiring on Recruitee + zero AI/ML conference talks + zero AI mentions on Glassdoor + zero AI press releases together makes a silent ML team unlikely. Recommend asking Eurail directly: 'How many people in the company have ML/AI/data-science as their primary title today?'

question: What is in the breach-leaked source code that journalists or researchers have reported on, beyond stack components?
searched: WebSearch breach reporting (BleepingComputer, SC Media, Recorded Future, SecurityWeek, Bright Defense, sqmagazine)
outcome: Journalists describe components stolen (S3, Zendesk, GitLab, source code, support tickets, database backups) but do not name specific ML models, AI services, or AI/ML libraries in code. No researcher has published a stack-analysis from the leaked code. Useful boundary: the public reporting confirms what kinds of artifacts were exfiltrated but not the AI/ML content of those artifacts.
